feed.title: Pronounce — recent dictionary additions
feed.subtitle: How engineers actually pronounce project / product / jargon names. Community-maintained.

entry.title: UXR — yoo ex ar
entry.summary: UXR is pronounced "yoo ex ar" (/ˌjuː ɛks ˈɑːr/). UX Researcher; letters "U-X-R".

entry.title: UXE — yoo ex ee
entry.summary: UXE is pronounced "yoo ex ee" (/ˌjuː ɛks ˈiː/). UX Engineer; letters "U-X-E".

entry.title: DS — dee ess
entry.summary: DS is pronounced "dee ess" (/ˌdiː ˈɛs/). Data Scientist; letters "D-S".

entry.title: DA — dee ay
entry.summary: DA is pronounced "dee ay" (/ˌdiː ˈeɪ/). Data Analyst; letters "D-A".

entry.title: DE — dee ee
entry.summary: DE is pronounced "dee ee" (/ˌdiː ˈiː/). Data Engineer; letters "D-E".

entry.title: DBA — dee bee ay
entry.summary: DBA is pronounced "dee bee ay" (/ˌdiː biː ˈeɪ/). Database Administrator; letters "D-B-A".

entry.title: BIE — bee eye ee
entry.summary: BIE is pronounced "bee eye ee" (/ˌbiː aɪ ˈiː/). Business Intelligence Engineer (Amazon title); letters "B-I-E".

entry.title: AE — ay ee
entry.summary: AE is pronounced "ay ee" (/ˌeɪ ˈiː/). Account Executive (sales); letters "A-E".

entry.title: AM — ay em
entry.summary: AM is pronounced "ay em" (/ˌeɪ ˈɛm/). Account Manager; letters "A-M".

entry.title: SE — ess ee
entry.summary: SE is pronounced "ess ee" (/ˌɛs ˈiː/). Sales/Solutions Engineer; letters "S-E".

entry.title: SA — ess ay
entry.summary: SA is pronounced "ess ay" (/ˌɛs ˈeɪ/). Solutions Architect; letters "S-A".

entry.title: CSM — see ess em
entry.summary: CSM is pronounced "see ess em" (/ˌsiː ɛs ˈɛm/). Customer Success Manager; letters "C-S-M".

entry.title: CS — see ess
entry.summary: CS is pronounced "see ess" (/ˌsiː ˈɛs/). Customer Success (also Computer Science); letters "C-S".

entry.title: SDR — ess dee ar
entry.summary: SDR is pronounced "ess dee ar" (/ˌɛs diː ˈɑːr/). Sales Development Rep; letters "S-D-R".

entry.title: BDR — bee dee ar
entry.summary: BDR is pronounced "bee dee ar" (/ˌbiː diː ˈɑːr/). Business Development Rep; letters "B-D-R".

entry.title: BD — bee dee
entry.summary: BD is pronounced "bee dee" (/ˌbiː ˈdiː/). Business Development; letters "B-D".

entry.title: BizOps — biz ops
entry.summary: BizOps is pronounced "biz ops" (/ˈbɪz ɒps/). Business Operations; "biz-ops".

entry.title: RevOps — rev ops
entry.summary: RevOps is pronounced "rev ops" (/ˈrɛv ɒps/). Revenue Operations; "rev-ops".

entry.title: PeopleOps — pee pul ops
entry.summary: PeopleOps is pronounced "pee pul ops" (/ˈpiːpəl ɒps/). People Operations (modern HR); "people-ops".

entry.title: HRBP — aitch ar bee pee
entry.summary: HRBP is pronounced "aitch ar bee pee" (/ˌeɪtʃ ɑːr biː ˈpiː/). HR Business Partner; letters "H-R-B-P".

entry.title: KR — kay ar
entry.summary: KR is pronounced "kay ar" (/ˌkeɪ ˈɑːr/). Key Result (the "KR" in OKR); letters "K-R".

entry.title: RACI — ray see
entry.summary: RACI is pronounced "ray see" (/ˈreɪsi/). Responsible-Accountable-Consulted-Informed; "RAY-see".

entry.title: EBR — ee bee ar
entry.summary: EBR is pronounced "ee bee ar" (/ˌiː biː ˈɑːr/). Executive Business Review; letters "E-B-R".

entry.title: MBR — em bee ar
entry.summary: MBR is pronounced "em bee ar" (/ˌɛm biː ˈɑːr/). Monthly Business Review; letters "M-B-R".

entry.title: MoTS — em oh tee ess
entry.summary: MoTS is pronounced "em oh tee ess" (/ˌɛm oʊ tiː ˈɛs/). Member of Technical Staff (compact form); letters or "mots".
